data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:43:30.362Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 9 ASP.NET Core web API to .NET 10. We have custom OpenAPI transformers that manipulate the document. After upgrading Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi to 10.0, we get compilation errors. Here's our code:

```csharp
using Microsoft.OpenApi.Any;
using Microsoft.OpenApi.Models;

options.AddOperationTransformer((operation, context, ct) =>
{
    var param = operation.Parameters.FirstOrDefault(p => p.Name == "api-version");
    if (param is not null)
    {
        param.Schema.Example = new OpenApiString("1.0");
    }

    operation.Responses.TryAdd("401", new OpenApiResponse { Description = "Unauthorized" });

    var oAuthScheme = new OpenApiSecurityScheme
    {
        Reference = new OpenApiReference { Type = ReferenceType.SecurityScheme, Id = "oauth2" }
    };
    operation.Security = new List<OpenApiSecurityRequirement>
    {
        new() { [oAuthScheme] = scopes }
    };

    return Task.CompletedTask;
});

options.AddSchemaTransformer((schema, context, ct) =>
{
    if (schema.Properties is not null)
    {
        foreach (var property in schema.Properties)
        {
            if (schema.Required?.Contains(property.Key) != true)
                property.Value.Nullable = false;
        }
    }
    return Task.CompletedTask;
});
```

What changed and how do I fix each piece?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about breaking changes when migrating from .NET 9 to .NET 10 in the Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi package. Let me research what changed in the OpenAPI library between these versions.

The key changes in .NET 10 / Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.0 relate to the upgrade from Microsoft.OpenApi v1.x to Microsoft.OpenApi v2.x. Let me look up the specific breaking changes.

Key breaking changes in Microsoft.OpenApi v2 (used by ASP.NET Core 10):

1. **`OpenApiAny` types removed** - `OpenApiString`, `OpenApiInteger`, etc. 

The `Example` property now uses `JsonNode` instead of `IOpenApiAny`. References and security schemes have also been restructured in v2, with `OpenApiReference` changing how it handles type and ID information. Schema definitions are likely affected as well. The reference types have been restructured too—I need to use specific reference classes like `OpenApiSchemaReference` or `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference` instead of the generic `OpenApiReference`. And since the `Nullable` property is gone, I should express nullability through the type array pattern that OpenAPI 3.1 uses. Let me verify the exact API changes to make sure I'm giving accurate guidance.

toolResult.textResultForLlm: Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/2.0.0: * [README](#readme-body-tab) * [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab) * [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab) * [Used By](#usedby-body-tab) * [Versions](#versions-body-tab) * [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab) ![Category overview screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/main/docs/images/oainet.png "Microsoft + OpenAPI = Love") ## OpenAPI.NET Package Nuget Models and Writers [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/) YamlReader [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader/) Hidi [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi/) The **OpenAPI.NET** SDK contains a useful object model for OpenAPI documents in .NET along with common serializers to extract raw OpenAPI JSON and YAML documents from the model. **See more information on the OpenAPI specification and its history here: OpenAPI Initiative** Project Objectives: * Provide a single shared object model in .NET for OpenAPI descriptions. * Include the most primitive Reader for ingesting OpenAPI JSON and YAML documents in both V2 and V3 formats. * Provide OpenAPI description writers for both V2 and V3 specification formats. * Enable developers to create Readers that translate different data formats into OpenAPI descriptions. ## Installation * Install core Nuget package [**Microsoft.OpenApi**](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi) * Install Yaml Reader Nuget package [**Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader**](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader) > Note: we just released a new major version of the library, which brings support for OpenAPI 3.1! You can read more about the changes of this upcoming version in the upgrade guide. ## Processors The OpenAPI.NET project holds the base object model for representing OpenAPI documents as .NET objects. Some developers have found the need to write processors that convert other data formats into this OpenAPI.NET object model. We'd like to curate that list of processors in this section of the readme. The base JSON and YAML processors are built into this project. Below is the list of the other supported processor projects. * [**C# Comment / Annotation Processor**](https://github.com/Microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.CSharpAnnotations) : Converts standard .NET annotations ( /// comments ) emitted from your build (MSBuild.exe) into OpenAPI.NET document object. * [**OData CSDL Processor**](https://github.com/Microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.OData) : Converts the XML representation of the Entity Data Model (EDM) describing an OData Service into OpenAPI.NET document object. ## Example Usage Creating an OpenAPI Document ``` var document = new OpenApiDocument { Info = new OpenApiInfo { Version = "1.0.0", Title = "Swagger Petstore (Simple)", }, Servers = new List { new OpenApiServer { Url = "http://petstore.swagger.io/api" } }, Paths = new OpenApiPaths { ["/pets"] = new OpenApiPathItem { Operations = new() { [HttpMethod.Get] = new OpenApiOperation { Description = "Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to", Responses = new OpenApiResponses { ["200"] = new OpenApiResponse { Description = "OK" } } } } } } }; ``` Reading and writing an OpenAPI description ``` var (openApiDocument, _) = await OpenApiDocument.LoadAsync("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/refs/heads/main/_archive_/schemas/v3.0/pass/petstore.yaml"); // Write V2 as JSON var outputString = await openApiDocument.SerializeAsJsonAsync(OpenApiSpecVersion.OpenApi2_0); ``` ## Validating/Testing OpenAPI descriptions In order to test the validity of an OpenApi document, we avail the following tools: * [Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi) A commandline tool for validating and transforming OpenAPI descriptions. [Installation guidelines and documentation](https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/blob/main/src/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi/readme.md) * Microsoft.OpenApi.Workbench A workbench tool consisting of a GUI where you can test and convert OpenAPI descriptions in both JSON and YAML from v2-->v3 and vice versa. ##### Installation guidelines: 1. Clone the repo locally by running this command: `git clone https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.git` 2. Open the solution file `(.sln)` in the root of the project with Visual Studio 3. Navigate to the `src/Microsoft.OpenApi.Workbench` directory and set it as the startup project 4. Run the project and you'll see a GUI pop up resembling the one below: ![workbench preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/main/docs/images/workbench.png "a screenshot of the workbench application") 5. Copy and paste your OpenAPI descriptions in the **Input Content** window or paste the path to the descriptions file in the **Input File** textbox and click on `Convert` to render the results. ## Contributing This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit [https://cla.microsoft.com](https://cla.microsoft.com/). When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., label, comment). Simply follow the instructions provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA. This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/). For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or contact opencode@microsoft.com with any additional questions or comments. To provide feedback and ask questions you can use Stack Overflow with the [OpenAPI.NET](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/openapi.net) tag. **Product** Compatible and additional computed convert OData CSDL to Open API Document of Model. 2.7M ### **GitHub repositories** (60) Showing the top 20 popular GitHub repositories that depend on Microsoft.OpenApi: Repository Stars A reference .NET application implementing an eCommerce site 10.5K [AssetRipper/AssetRipper](https://github.com/AssetRipper/AssetRipper) GUI Application to work with engine assets, asset bundles, and serialized files 7.5K [microsoft/aspire](https://github.com/microsoft/aspire) Aspire is the tool for code-first, extensible, observable dev and deploy. 5.9K Based on WireMock Java, but extended and different functionality. Full documentation can be found at https://wiremock.org/dotnet/. 1.7K [smartstore/Smartstore](https://github.com/smartstore/Smartstore) A modular, scalable and ultra-fast open-source all-in-one eCommerce platform built on ASP.NET Core 10 1.5K [Azure/data-api-builder](https://github.com/Azure/data-api-builder) Data API builder provides modern REST, GraphQL endpoints and MCP tools to your Azure Databases and on-prem stores. 1.4K [CoreUnion/CoreShop](https://github.com/CoreUnion/CoreShop) 基于 Asp.Net Core 9.0、Uni-App开发，支持可视化布局的小程序商城系统，前后端分离，支持分布式部署，跨平台运行，拥有分销、代理、团购、拼团、秒杀、直播、优惠券、自定义表单等众多营销功能，拥有完整SKU、下单、售后、物流流程。支持一套代码编译发布微信小程序版、H5版、Android版、iOS版、支付宝小程序版、字节跳动小程序版、QQ小程序版等共10个平台。 1.4K [VirtoCommerce/vc-platform](https://github.com/VirtoCommerce/vc-platform) Virto Commerce B2B Innovation Platform 1.3K [aspnet/AspLabs](https://github.com/aspnet/AspLabs) Repo for ASP.NET experiments that are not ready for a production release 1.2K [dotnet/dotnet](https://github.com/dotnet/dotnet) Home of .NET's Virtual Monolithic Repository which includes all the code needed to build the .NET SDK. 1.1K [MUnique/OpenMU](https://github.com/MUnique/OpenMU) This project aims to create an easy to use, extendable and customizable server for a MMORPG called "MU Online". 1.1K Include prerelease Include vulnerable Include deprecated Version Downloads Last Updated [3.5.3](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.5.3 "3.5.3") 90,644 4/27/2026 [3.5.2](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.5.2 "3.5.2") 82,821 4/14/2026 [3.5.1](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.5.1 "3.5.1") 136,191 3/31/2026 [3.5.0](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.5.0 "3.5.0") 74,056 3/20/2026 [3.4.0](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.4.0 "3.4.0") 122,388 3/5/2026 [3.3.1](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.3.1 "3.3.1") 317,631 1/22/2026 [3.3.0](/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/3.3.0 "3.3.0") 25,809 1/21/2026 3.3.0 is deprecated because it has critical bugs. result.detailedContent: Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/2.0.0: * [README](#readme-body-tab) * [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab) * [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab) * [Used By](#usedby-body-tab) * [Versions](#versions-body-tab) * [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab) ![Category overview screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/main/docs/images/oainet.png "Microsoft + OpenAPI = Love") ## OpenAPI.NET Package Nuget Models and Writers [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi/) YamlReader [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader/) Hidi [![nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi/) The **OpenAPI.NET** SDK contains a useful object model for OpenAPI documents in .NET along with common serializers to extract raw OpenAPI JSON and YAML documents from the model. **See more information on the OpenAPI specification and its history here: OpenAPI Initiative** Project Objectives: * Provide a single shared object model in .NET for OpenAPI descriptions. * Include the most primitive Reader for ingesting OpenAPI JSON and YAML documents in both V2 and V3 formats. * Provide OpenAPI description writers for both V2 and V3 specification formats. * Enable developers to create Readers that translate different data formats into OpenAPI descriptions. ## Installation * Install core Nuget package [**Microsoft.OpenApi**](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi) * Install Yaml Reader Nuget package [**Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader**](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.YamlReader) > Note: we just released a new major version of the library, which brings support for OpenAPI 3.1! You can read more about the changes of this upcoming version in the upgrade guide. ## Processors The OpenAPI.NET project holds the base object model for representing OpenAPI documents as .NET objects. Some developers have found the need to write processors that convert other data formats into this OpenAPI.NET object model. We'd like to curate that list of processors in this section of the readme. The base JSON and YAML processors are built into this project. Below is the list of the other supported processor projects. * [**C# Comment / Annotation Processor**](https://github.com/Microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.CSharpAnnotations) : Converts standard .NET annotations ( /// comments ) emitted from your build (MSBuild.exe) into OpenAPI.NET document object. * [**OData CSDL Processor**](https://github.com/Microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.OData) : Converts the XML representation of the Entity Data Model (EDM) describing an OData Service into OpenAPI.NET document object. ## Example Usage Creating an OpenAPI Document ``` var document = new OpenApiDocument { Info = new OpenApiInfo { Version = "1.0.0", Title = "Swagger Petstore (Simple)", }, Servers = new List { new OpenApiServer { Url = "http://petstore.swagger.io/api" } }, Paths = new OpenApiPaths { ["/pets"] = new OpenApiPathItem { Operations = new() { [HttpMethod.Get] = new OpenApiOperation { Description = "Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to", Responses = new OpenApiResponses { ["200"] = new OpenApiResponse { Description = "OK" } } } } } } }; ``` Reading and writing an OpenAPI description ``` var (openApiDocument, _) = await OpenApiDocument.LoadAsync("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/refs/heads/main/_archive_/schemas/v3.0/pass/petstore.yaml"); // Write V2 as JSON var outputString = await openApiDocument.SerializeAsJsonAsync(OpenApiSpecVersion.OpenApi2_0); ``` ## Validating/Testing OpenAPI descriptions In order to test the validity of an OpenApi document, we avail the following tools: * [Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi) A commandline tool for validating and transforming OpenAPI descriptions. [Installation guidelines and documentation](https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/blob/main/src/Microsoft.OpenApi.Hidi/readme.md) * Microsoft.OpenApi.Workbench A workbench tool consisting of a GUI where you can test and convert OpenAPI descriptions in both JSON and YAML from v2-->v3 and vice versa. ##### Installation guidelines: 1. Clone the repo locally by running this command: `git clone https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET.git` 2. Open the solution file `(.sln)` in the root of the project with Visual Studio 3. Navigate to the `src/Microsoft.OpenApi.Workbench` directory and set it as the startup project 4. Run the project and you'll see a GUI pop up resembling the one below: ![workbench preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/main/docs/images/workbench.png "a screenshot of the workbench application") 5. Copy and paste your OpenAPI descriptions in the **Input Content** window or paste the path to the descriptions file in the **Input File** textbox and click on `Convert` to render the results. ## Contributing This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit [https://cla.microsoft.com](https://cla.microsoft.com/). When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., label, comment). Simply follow the instructions provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA. This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/). For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or contact opencode@microsoft.com with any additional questions or comments. To provide feedback and ask questions you can use Stack Overflow with the [OpenAPI.NET](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/openapi.net) tag. **Product** Compatible and additional computed data.reasoningText:
Now I have very good information. Let me look at the key changes from the official docs. The Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.0 uses Microsoft.OpenApi v2.x (or later - looks like it could be v2.x or v3.x based on the nuget page showing versions up to 3.5.3). Let me look more carefully at the docs example.

From the docs, I can see the key changes in the .NET 10 OpenAPI transformers:

1. **Security scheme references** - Now use `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference` instead of `OpenApiSecurityScheme` with a `Reference` property:


Creating security scheme references with the new reference type...

2. **`IOpenApiSecurityScheme` interface** - Security schemes now use the interface `IOpenApiSecurityScheme` in dictionaries:
Updating security scheme dictionaries...

3. **Schema.Example** - Uses `JsonNode` instead of `IOpenApiAny`/`OpenApiString`, and in OpenAPI 3.1 (the default in .NET 10), the `nullable` property works differently than in earlier versions.

4. **OpenApiResponses** - The `Add` method still functions as expected based on the documentation.

Looking at the `AuthOperationTransformer` example, I see the docs still show the older pattern with `OpenApiSecurityScheme` and `Reference`, though they seem to be encouraging the newer approach. But examining it more closely, there's a discrepancy in how the examples are structured.

The operation transformer section demonstrates the traditional `OpenApiSecurityScheme { Reference = ... }` approach, while the document transformer section uses the newer `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference("Bearer", document)` constructor. Since the user is hitting compilation errors, the real culprits are likely that `OpenApiString` has been removed entirely in favor of `JsonNode`, and the `OpenApiReference` pattern with `Type = ReferenceType.SecurityScheme` may no longer be valid or has changed.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the changes. Let me also check the first page I fetched more carefully, specifically the BearerSecuritySchemeTransformer where it uses `IOpenApiSecurityScheme` interface and `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference`.

From my research, the key findings for .NET 10 (Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.0 + Microsoft.OpenApi v2.x):

1. **`OpenApiString` and other `OpenApiAny` types are removed** - `Schema.Example` now uses `JsonNode` (from `System.Text.Json.Nodes`) instead of `IOpenApiAny`.


2. **`SecuritySchemes` dictionary type changed** - I'm seeing inconsistencies between the documentation pages. The first version shows `Dictionary<string, IOpenApiSecurityScheme>` with `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference`, while the second shows `Dictionary<string, OpenApiSecurityScheme>`. I need to look more carefully at which version is correct.

Comparing the two patterns... In OpenAPI 3.1, the `Nullable` property approach has shifted—instead of a boolean flag, nullability is now expressed by adding "null" to the type array in the schema. The `Responses` collection still follows the same pattern for adding responses to operations.

The main breaking changes are that `OpenApiString`, `OpenApiInteger`, and other types from `Microsoft.OpenApi.Any` have been removed in v2, so I need to use `JsonValue.Create()` from `System.Text.Json.Nodes` instead. For security schemes, I'm still working through whether the old reference pattern still works or if there's a new approach required. doesn't exist in v2/v3, and `new OpenApiString("1.0")` was removed entirely. The `Nullable` property on schema objects also changed with the shift to OpenAPI 3.1's type array model. I need to figure out which version of Microsoft.OpenApi the AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.0 package actually depends on to understand what's available.

Looking at the NuGet history, Microsoft.OpenApi has evolved from 1.6.x through 2.x and into 3.x versions, and since .NET 10 is recent, it's likely using Microsoft.OpenApi 2.x. The major breaking changes between 1.x and 2.x include removing the entire `Microsoft.OpenApi.Any` namespace—types like `OpenApiString`, `OpenApiInteger`, and `OpenApiBoolean` are gone, replaced with `JsonNode` for handling examples, defaults, and enums. The `Nullable` property was also removed in favor of OpenAPI 3.1's approach of including `"null"` in the type collection.

Reference handling changed significantly too, with new type-safe reference classes like `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference` and `OpenApiSchemaReference` introduced. I'm checking whether the old pattern of creating references with `Type` and `Id` properties still works or if it's been completely replaced by the new constructor-based approach I'm seeing in the documentation.
